text: iptables (legacy) and nftables (modern, since kernel 3.13) are Linux firewall frameworks based on Netfilter hooks. nftables replaces iptables with unified syntax, better performance, and IPv4/IPv6 handling. Used to create packet filtering rules (allow/deny traffic).
nftables: `nft add table inet filter`, `nft add chain inet filter input { type filter hook input priority 0; }`, `nft add rule inet filter input tcp dport 22 accept`.
